title: AI Builder Brief Configuration
description: User configuration for the ai-builder-brief skill. Stored at ~/.ai-builder-brief/config.json

mode.description: Personal mode: briefing tailored to one person's role and interests. Team mode: briefing for a company/team with shared context.

identity.description: Who you are and why AI matters to you. Drives the 'so what' framing for every story.

name.description: Your first name. Used to personalize the briefing header and subject line (e.g., 'AI Builder Brief for Luna')

description.description: One sentence about your role (personal) or team context (team). e.g., 'I'm a PM at a B2B SaaS startup, adding AI features to our product'

why.description: Why AI intel matters to you. Shapes what gets surfaced and how it's framed. e.g., 'I need to track which AI tools my peers are adopting'

company.description: Company details (team mode only). Omit for personal mode.

description.description: What the company does, in one sentence

decisions.description: What decisions readers make based on this intel

focus.description: AI topic areas to track (e.g., ['AI agents', 'AI developer tools', 'AI regulation'])

focusReasons.description: Maps each focus area to a reason string explaining why it matters. Keys should match items in focus[].

categories.description: News categories to monitor. Each gets its own section in the briefing.

searchHints.description: Additional search terms for this category

default.name: AI Products & Tools
default.description: New AI product launches, feature updates, pricing changes

default.name: AI Builders & Trends
default.description: What top AI practitioners are saying, building, and shipping

default.name: AI Funding & Launches
default.description: AI startup funding rounds, acquisitions, new company launches

default.name: AI Research & Models
default.description: New model releases, benchmark results, research breakthroughs

default.name: AI Regulation & Policy
default.description: AI governance, regulation, safety, and policy developments

competitors.description: Specific competitors to watch (optional, primarily for team mode)

watchList.description: People and organizations to actively track for AI-related activity

people.description: Names of individuals to watch (e.g., 'Sam Altman', 'Dario Amodei')

orgs.description: Organizations to watch beyond competitors (e.g., 'OpenAI', 'Anthropic', 'Y Combinator')

curated.description: Specific sources to always check (newsletters, blogs, podcasts)

preferredDomains.description: Domains to prefer in search results (e.g., ['techcrunch.com', 'theverge.com'])

method.description: How to deliver the briefing. 'resend' sends via Resend API. 'resend+local' sends and saves a local copy.

recipients.description: Email addresses to receive the briefing

resend.description: Resend API configuration. Required when method is 'resend' or 'resend+local'.

envFile.description: Path to .env file containing the Resend API key

envVar.description: Environment variable name for the Resend API key

fromAddress.description: Sender email address (must be verified in Resend)

driveSearch.description: Optional Google Drive integration for internal context
